audit_items.proof_trace: The inline functions in rio.h (rioWrite, rioRead, rioTell, rioFlush) never free any pointer; all function pointers they invoke are set from static const structures (rioBufferIO, rioFileIO, rioFdsetIO) with process lifetime. The only freeing of rio-owned heap data is rioFreeFdset (rio.c:280-284), which has exactly one call site (rdb.c:2371) immediately followed by exitFromChild — no rio accessor can be invoked after the free.
